Hmm, why did I perform so average? Maybe the hours I chose weren't optimal. Let's think about peak times. Usually, delivery demand is higher during lunch (around noon) and dinner (late afternoon). So maybe starting earlier could help me catch more orders in the morning rush.

Starting at 7 AM instead of 8 might let me cover more early orders before others even start. Then, if I finish a bit later, say 6:30 PM, perhaps I can capture some evening orders that come after the typical 6 PM cutoff.

Alternatively, maybe starting earlier gives me a head start on the busiest times, allowing me to pick up more orders and earn more money. If I can cover the peak periods better, my earnings might increase compared to others who started later.

I should adjust my hours to 7 AM start and finish at 6:30 PM or maybe 7 PM. Let's see which makes sense without getting too tired. Maybe 7 AM to 7 PM gives me a full day with the peak times covered, but that's 12 hours. Alternatively, starting earlier and finishing slightly later than yesterday might be more efficient.

I think shifting my start time by an hour and extending the end time by half an hour could give me better coverage during high-demand periods, potentially improving my rankings today.

Alternatively, if I extend my shift later into the evening, I can capture the dinner rush, which is typically a high-order period. That could boost both my earnings and order count significantly.

Perhaps the best approach is a slight adjustment rather than a drastic one. I think a balanced approach would be better. Starting an hour earlier and finishing an hour later might give me that extra edge needed to capture more orders during both peaks without significantly increasing my total working time beyond what's manageable.

But considering the initial data, perhaps I should try starting at 6:00 AM instead of 7:00 AM and finish at 21:00 instead of 20:00. That would give me an additional hour in both the morning and evening peaks, potentially increasing my order count and earnings.
